AI Vision-Based Reference Selection Refactor
Goal Description
Enhance the existing 
ship-reference-service.ts
 to utilize an AI Vision-capable model (via our llm-gateway) to evaluate and select the optimal ship reference images obtained from Google SerpAPI. This replaces an over-reliance on exact text/keyword matching heuristics, ensuring the chosen image truly represents the ship, context, and aesthetic quality needed. Furthermore, we will align this with the new 
IMAGE_GOVERNANCE_SELECTION_SYSTEM.md
 by using the AI to initialize intelligent curation tags directly on the asset records.

Proposed Changes
1. 
lib/campaigns/schema.ts
Update types to capture the AI Vision analysis.

[MODIFY] 
ShipReferenceCandidate
: Add optional fields for aiScore, aiReasoning, detectedTags, and antiTags.
[MODIFY] 
AssetRecord
 (if necessary): Ensure the generated suitabilityTags and antiTags can be cleanly persisted from the AI's deductions.
2. lib/campaigns/media/vision-evaluator.ts
[NEW] Create a standalone module to handle the Vision LLM calls.
Logic:
Will receive a batch of candidate image URLs, the campaign.shipTarget, and the intended category (exterior, pool_deck, dining, etc.).
Prompt instructions will ask the Vision model to assess:
Is this actually a cruise ship (and likely the referenced class/ship)?
Does it visually match the requested category?
Are there disqualifying attributes (e.g., CGI, render, generic hotel, blurry, text overlay)?
Returns structured JSON containing a score (1-100), reasoning, and inferred tags.
3. 
lib/campaigns/media/ship-reference-service.ts
[MODIFY] 
discoverShipReferenceCandidatesWithExclusions
:
Fetch candidates from searchGoogleImages as normal.
Run the existing text-based filters (
classifyReferenceMatchLevel
, 
shouldHardRejectReferenceCandidate
) as a cheap pre-filter to weed out obvious junk.
Take the remaining N candidates and pass them to the new vision-evaluator.
Re-score and sort candidates heavily weighting the AI Vision score over the rudimentary text overlap score.
[MODIFY] 
buildExternalReferenceAssetRecord
 & 
importCandidateAsAsset
:
Pass the AI's detectedTags into the record's target tags (suitabilityTags etc. depending on how curation is stored currently).
Add the AI's reasoning to curatorNotes or a similar metadata field so the system operator has context for why the AI allowed the image.
4. 
lib/ai/llm-gateway/models.ts
 & 
lib/ai/llm-gateway/index.ts
[MODIFY] (If Needed) Ensure that an appropriate vision-capable model (like GPT_5_HIGH / gpt-4o) is mapped for a vision_evaluation task.
User Review Required
IMPORTANT

Which specific LLM model should we route the vision requests to? (e.g., GPT_5_HIGH or CLAUDE_4_OPUS)
Do you want to evaluate candidate images individually, or batch them in a single prompt for relative ranking (to save time/costs)?
Should this apply to all reference categories (exterior, pool deck, dining, stateroom), or specifically just the Hero exterior references?
Verification Plan
Automated / Integration
None strictly required beyond standard compilation.
Manual Verification
Navigate to the /tests/media-generation route.
Enter a slug for a cruise campaign (e.g., representing "Royal Caribbean Icon of the Seas").
Enable preflight and click GENERATE BY CATEGORY -> References.
Verify the server logs to ensure the AI Vision evaluator is invoked.
Inspect the generated manifest in the UI and confirm the top downloaded reference images are accurate and visually optimal.
Validate the populated asset curation tags (suitabilityTags, antiTags).
